Mitra.texts: Rig Veda, Taittiriya Samhita, Shatapatha Brahmana
Mitra.desc: The deity of oaths, divine friendship, and universal contracts.

Varuna.texts: Rig Veda, Shatapatha Brahmana, Atharva Veda
Varuna.desc: The cosmic lord of waters, truth (Rita), and moral law.

Aryaman.texts: Rig Veda, Atharva Veda, Taittiriya Brahmana
Aryaman.desc: The deity of customs, hospitality, and sacred marriages.

Bhaga.desc: The divine distributor of wealth, luck, and prosperity.

Amsha.desc: The deity representing the rightful share or portion of gods and men.

Dhatri.desc: The divine creator, sustainer, and deity of health and magic.

Indra (Shakra).desc: The king of the gods, master of storms, rain, and martial power.

Vivasvat (Surya).texts: Rig Veda, Surya Siddhanta, Aditya Hrudayam
Vivasvat (Surya).desc: The embodiment of the sun and the radiant progenitor of humanity.

Tvashthri.desc: The divine architect, creator of forms, and blacksmith of the gods.

Pushan.desc: The deity of meetings, safe journeys, and protector of travelers and cattle.

Savitri.texts: Rig Veda, Gayatri Mantra, Yajur Veda
Savitri.desc: The golden sun before sunrise, deity of divine inspiration and the sacred word.

Vishnu.texts: Rig Veda, Vishnu Purana, Bhagavata Purana
Vishnu.desc: The all-pervading preserver and sustainer of the cosmos.

Kapalin.desc: The skull-bearing, supreme ascetic form of cosmic dissolution.

Pingala.desc: The golden-hued, fiery aspect of the divine storm.

Bhima.desc: The tremendously powerful, awe-inspiring, and terrible aspect.

Virupaksha.desc: The one with innumerable eyes, the all-seeing cosmic witness.

Vilohita.desc: The deep red, ferocious manifestation of divine energy.

Ajaikapad.texts: Rig Veda, Atharva Veda, Taittiriya Samhita
Ajaikapad.desc: The one-footed deity representing the vertical axis of the universe and spiritual fire.

Ahirbudhnya.desc: The serpent of the deep atmospheric ocean and protector of the sacred fire.

Shasta.desc: The supreme teacher, ruler, and lawgiver of the universe.

Shambhu.desc: The benevolent, peaceful, and auspicious bestower of happiness.

Chanda.desc: The fiercely wrathful and unyielding punisher of Adharma.

Bhava.desc: The principle of existence, life, and the ultimate protector.

Dhara (Prithvi).texts: Rig Veda, Atharva Veda (Bhumi Sukta), Vishnu Purana
Dhara (Prithvi).desc: The goddess of the earth, the foundation and support of all physical life.

Anala (Agni).desc: The god of fire, the eternal witness, and the messenger between humans and gods.

Anila (Vayu).desc: The god of wind, representing the cosmic breath (Prana) and life force.

Aha (Antariksha).desc: The deity of space, water, and the all-pervading atmosphere.

Pratyusha.desc: The deity of the pre-dawn light, responsible for dispersing the darkness of the night.

Prabhasa.desc: The deity of the dawn and the shining, splendid sky.

Soma (Chandra).desc: The god of the moon, the elixir of immortality, and the nourisher of flora.

Dhruva.desc: The steadfast Pole Star, the ultimate symbol of perseverance and fixed cosmic order.

Nasatya.desc: The divine twin physician, restorer of health, youth, and the bringer of miracles.

Dasra.desc: The divine twin physician, bestower of wondrous remedies and the light of dawn.
